evidence: docs/README.md says no canonical docs/verification or release-evidence route exists yet and assigns bounded proof ownership to HGI-203 and operational routing to HGI-204.
packages/scripts/README.md records command stdout and stderr in an invocation outcome but does not define full-log retention, redaction, canonical storage, access control, or deletion policy.
The source receipt found no canonical release-proof or runbook route.
rejectedAlternatives: Retain all full command logs indefinitely in the repository.
Redact or delete logs using an unstated convention.
Treat an existing documentation-audit receipt as the canonical release proof packet or full-log store.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: No full-log or proof-packet retention, redaction, or location policy is established. HGI-203 and HGI-204 must design and obtain maintainer acceptance for the minimal required proof fields, sensitive-data handling, canonical location, access boundary, retention and deletion schedule, integrity/readback, and recovery before claiming durable release evidence.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: A local check outcome is not a governed retained proof packet or full-log record.
A release/recovery runbook cannot promise evidence retention or redaction without a policy.
Automation cannot enforce retention or redaction criteria that have not been chosen.

authorityImpact: Retention, redaction, access, and deletion decisions may affect sensitive operational data and require the named maintainers; this record authorizes no evidence movement or deletion.
nonClaims: No full release logs, proof packets, retention period, redaction rule, canonical evidence location, access boundary, or deletion policy is established.
No release, publication, registry, deployment, production, or external-consumer state is proved.
carryingCost: Preserve the absence as an explicit blocker so future proof or runbook work does not silently create a retention policy.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: The TaxKit release and verification maintainers approve a bounded evidence schema, redaction classification, canonical location, access model, retention/deletion schedule, integrity readback, and recovery procedure.
